_why: THE EXTENSION PROXY SEAM HAS TWO IMPLEMENTATIONS, AND THEY MUST NOT DRIFT.
An extension declares the data-plane routes its UI needs in extensions/<name>/ui/canvas/
canvas_proxy.json. That manifest is read TWICE: by vite.config.ts in development, and by
the ACE canvas host (core/engine/api/canvas_host.py) in production. Same declaration, two
Two implementations of a fail-closed merge is exactly the shape of bug that ships: dev
refuses a collision, prod silently allows it, and one extension's traffic is quietly routed
into another extension's data plane. Both services answer 200. The numbers are simply
someone else's, and nothing anywhere says so.
So the RULES live here, as data, and both implementations are tested against this file.
Change the behaviour in one language and the other language's test goes red.

cases.name: a declared prefix is merged, and target survives
targetEnv overrides target when set — an operator repoints without a commit
an EMPTY targetEnv is not an override — it is an unset variable
two prefixes from one extension both merge
REFUSES to shadow a kernel route
cases._why: A stray /health entry silently swallows the kernel's own health route, and the canvas starts asking a data service whether ACE is alive.

cases.name: REFUSES two extensions claiming one prefix
cases._why: Last-wins routes one extension's traffic into another's data plane. Both answer 200; the numbers are someone else's.

cases.name: REFUSES a prefix that is not a path
